seoTitle: How to Apply a Credit Note to an Invoice in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Learn how to apply a credit note to an invoice in QuickBooks so refunds and adjustments reduce the customer's balance accurately and keep your books clean.
intro: When a customer is owed a credit, applying it correctly to an open invoice keeps your accounts receivable accurate. This guide walks through how to link a credit note to an invoice in QuickBooks so the balance reflects the adjustment properly.

steps.heading: Create or locate the credit note
steps.body: Find the existing credit note or create a new one for the customer whose invoice needs adjusting.

steps.heading: Open the customer's invoice
steps.body: Go to the invoice you want to apply the credit against, making sure it belongs to the same customer as the credit note.

steps.heading: Apply the credit
steps.body: Use the receive payment or apply credit option to link the credit note amount to the invoice balance.

steps.heading: Verify the updated balance
steps.body: Check that the invoice now reflects the reduced amount owed after the credit has been applied.

faq.question: Can a credit note be applied to multiple invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, if the credit amount is larger than one invoice, you can split it across multiple open invoices for the same customer.

faq.question: What happens if I don't apply the credit note?
faq.answer: An unapplied credit note will sit as an open credit on the customer's account and won't reduce any invoice balance until applied.

faq.question: Can I undo an applied credit note?
faq.answer: Yes, you can unlink or edit the applied credit if you made a mistake, though it's best to double-check before saving.

faq.question: Does applying a credit note affect sales tax?
faq.answer: It can, depending on how the original invoice and credit note were set up, so review the tax lines after applying it.
